concerns.text: Claim 3 ('Roughly X% of 'correct' answers...') uses a placeholder 'X' for a primary empirical finding. The paper spec must not promise a specific statistical finding that the research has not yet produced or quantified. This is HARKing-by-spec risk; the spec must either state the actual measured value from the research artifacts or explicitly frame the claim as a hypothesis to be tested, not a reported result.

Claim 1 ('Standard accuracy metrics fail to penalize...') asserts a definitive failure of standard metrics based on the research. The spec must verify that the research actually measured 'standard accuracy' alongside 'SAA' to make this comparative claim. If the research only measured SAA, this claim is an over-reach beyond the produced data.

Figure 2 ('Error Distribution Bar Chart') and Figure 3 ('Dataset Integrity & Skipped Records') are mandated as required figures. The spec must confirm that the research actually generated the specific data breakdowns (answer_only_correct, region_only_correct, skipped reasons) necessary to render these charts. If the research only produced a single aggregate score, these figures are impossible to generate without fabricating data.
